claim: Over 50,000 galgos are destroyed annually by galguero hunters in Spain, primarily at the end of the one-season hunting cycle — producing a large population of recently traumatized/abandoned individuals in the Iberian peninsula.
stance_target: galgo trauma baseline in Iberian/Algarve population is high by structural necessity, not individual variation
quoted_support: There is a very cruel and barbaric custom amongst many Spanish hunters, or 'Galgueros,' that destroys more than 50,000 Galgos every year.

claim: Rescued galgos show overt trauma behavior immediately visible at intake — one rescued galga described as 'very frightened and had clearly suffered at the hands of her Galguero.'
stance_target: galgo trauma baseline is observable at intake and directly shapes behavior in lost/rescue scenarios
scope_conditions: galgos rescued from galguero abandonment in Spain
quoted_support: This Galga was very frightened and had clearly suffered at the hands of her Galguero.
